# Machine Learning Fundamentals

## Definition

Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn from data.
ML algorithms improve their performance through experience without being explicitly programmed.

## Types of Machine Learning

### Supervised Learning
Supervised learning uses labeled training data to learn a mapping from inputs to outputs.
Common supervised learning algorithms include linear regression, decision trees, and neural networks.
Classification assigns inputs to discrete categories.
Regression predicts continuous numerical values.

### Unsupervised Learning
Unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns in data without labeled examples.
Clustering groups similar data points together.
K-means is a popular clustering algorithm that partitions data into k groups.
Dimensionality reduction reduces the number of features in a dataset.

### Reinforcement Learning
Reinforcement learning trains agents to make decisions by rewarding desired behaviors.
An agent learns by interacting with an environment and receiving feedback as rewards or penalties.

## Neural Networks

A neural network consists of layers of interconnected nodes called neurons.
The input layer receives raw data.
Hidden layers transform the data through learned weights.
The output layer produces the final prediction.
Deep learning uses neural networks with many hidden layers.
Backpropagation is the algorithm used to train neural networks by adjusting weights.

## Evaluation Metrics

Accuracy measures the proportion of correct predictions out of all predictions.
Precision measures how many predicted positives are actually positive.
Recall measures how many actual positives were correctly identified.
The F1 score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall.

## Key Concepts

Overfitting occurs when a model learns the training data too well and fails to generalize.
Regularization techniques like L1 and L2 help prevent overfitting.
Cross-validation splits data into multiple folds to evaluate model performance reliably.
A training set is used to fit the model, a validation set for hyperparameter tuning, and a test set for final evaluation.
